title: Hearing complaint
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped hearing-difficulty, sudden or rapid change, wax, infection, audiology, and urgent referral fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment

examination.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped unilateral, tinnitus, vertigo, neurological, ear pain, discharge, abnormal-ear, and clinician-arranged referral fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral

treatment_and_management.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports mapped history, communication, function, otoscopy, audiology, hearing-device discussion, questions, support, and follow-up fields.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source_limitations: Children, ear fullness, noise exposure specificity, recent trauma, whisper or tuning-fork tests, head trauma, and fixed two-week result-review timing remain unsupported.
Conductive or sensorineural classification remains clinician-stated and test dependent.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
